subject:
Re: I create an artifact map of teamtomo.org EMPIAR-10164 dataset
 in 1.6Apx
body:
Thank you very much! Was the slice I show in the symmetry_axis_align right? 
The map was in the last 6th dialogue.

your map was so good. 
I am re-running the findparticles dcp job again now. Wait some time and I 
will show the result.

btw, can I still use the relion 3.0.6? Maybe the star file should be 
revised to use relion 3.1. Can you teach me how to handle it? Thanks a lot. 
在2021年6月21日星期一 UTC+8 下午8:31:24<lorenzo...@gmail.com> 写道：

> The relion version should not be a problem here.
>
> I think that the map you showed above probably comes from a wrongly 
> aligned set of particles. For reference, here's the same image from my 
> processing:
> [image: image.png]
>
> Differently from your map, this has well-defined cylindrical holes going 
> through several slices where there's very little density (=much darker).
> I think that the patterns you see in your data are simply caused by 
> imposing symmetry on noisy data. I would do as Alister suggested, and go 
> back a couple of steps and re-do the find-particles section to make sure 
> that you are indeed using the initial model with symmetry axis aligned to 
> the center.
>
> Best,
> Lorenzo
>
> On Mon, Jun 21, 2021 at 1:45 PM 'yuqi zhang' via Warp <
> war...@googlegroups.com> wrote:
>
>> The picture was broken. I updated them.
>>
>> 在2021年6月21日星期一 UTC+8 下午7:43:11<yuqi zhang> 写道：
>>
>>>   The random_subset_5Apx.mrc was noiser.
>>>
>>>
>>> [image: 1624275233(1).jpg]
>>> [image: 1624275260(1).png]
>>>
>>> One potential problem was the relion version.
>>>
>>> In the teamtomo.org, I saw the relion background color was purple 
>>> rather than orange. So it must be relion 3.1 or later.
>>>
>>> I used the relion 3.0.6 rather than 3.1.1.
>>> The generated star file in WARP was an old version.
>>> When I used this star file in relion 3.1.1, it report an error:
>>>
>>> [image: 1624275734(1).png]
>>>
>>> I was so sorry to ask a relion question in warp discussion group.
>>>
>>> 在2021年6月21日星期一 UTC+8 下午7:23:48<lorenzo...@gmail.com> 写道：
>>>
>>>> Hi Yuqi,
>>>>
>>>> The error you see is a bug in the script, but should not a problem for 
>>>> you (the thing that's failing is just for visualization). We'll have to fix 
>>>> that though!
>>>> (EDIT: I just saw you spotted it as well!)
>>>>
>>>> If indeed you aligned the symmetry using the script and proceeded with 
>>>> the aligned initial model, then the problem must be later down the line.
>>>> When maps are very noisy, it's often better to look at them in slices, 
>>>> rather than isosurfaces.
>>>>
>>>> How does your `random_subset_5Apx.mrc` look like if you open it with 
>>>> `dview('random_subset_5Apx.mrc')` in matlab?
>>>> It should look similar to the initial model pictures we were sharing 
>>>> earlier. If it does not, you then we know the problem is somewhere in 
>>>> between. If it does, we can check some other things later on.
>>>>
>>>> Anyways, did you do as suggested by Amédée (lowpass filter the map 
>>>> before looking at it as isosurface)? 
>>>>
>>>> Best,
>>>> Lorenzo
>>>>
>>>> On Mon, Jun 21, 2021 at 1:15 PM 'yuqi zhang' via Warp <
>>>> war...@googlegroups.com> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> To display the results, I change the command line like this in 
>>>>> *highlight*
>>>>> [image: 1624273900(1).png]
>>>>>
>>>>> And then, the 4 dview map were there:
>>>>> [image: 1624273765(1).jpg]
>>>>> [image: 1624273800(1).png]
>>>>> [image: 1624273814(1).png]
>>>>> [image: 1624273875(1).png]
>>>>>
>>>>> Are these results right for whole dataset alignment?
>>>>> 在2021年6月21日星期一 UTC+8 下午6:53:20<yuqi zhang> 写道：
>>>>>
>>>>>> Besides only run the script "align_symmetry_axis", I didn't know any 
>>>>>> other thing I should do.
>>>>>> 在2021年6月21日星期一 UTC+8 下午6:43:14<yuqi zhang> 写道：
>>>>>>
>>>>>>> When I run the script "align_symmetry_axis". There was an error 
>>>>>>> information:
>>>>>>> [image: 1624271983(1).png]
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> So I do the "%" to this command line. And in the left column, there 
>>>>>>> was the result in the folder "inimodel/results/ite_0008averages/".
>>>>>>> I think the "%" may be OK. Anyway, you can tell me how to debug for 
>>>>>>> this if you like.
>>>>>>> 在2021年6月21日星期一 UTC+8 下午6:29:08<alist...@gmail.com> 写道：
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Hi Yuqi,
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> The result below shows that you aligned to a reference which was 
>>>>>>>> not centered on the 6-fold axis - this is where your problems come from, 
>>>>>>>> same advice from my previous message applies :)
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Cheers,
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Alister
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> And then, I continued to* align all particles*.
>>>>>>>> The folder *findallData.Data* contains *31561* particles  *without 
>>>>>>>> dBox mode*. 
>>>>>>>> I used the dcp in dynamo and the result was like this:
>>>>>>>> [image: 1624031060(1).png][image: 1624032003(1).jpg]
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> --
